notes:
Gym Challenge (gym2), WotC Gym series, released 2000 — pre-2002 early WotC, so NO reverse-holo slot. Booster = 11 cards. Bulbapedia confirms 11-card boosters for all WotC sets Base through Neo Destiny (which includes the Gym series). Composition per pack: 1 rare-or-better, 3 uncommon, 7 common. Holo insertion rate ~1 in 3 packs is the well-documented WotC-era baseline (Flipside Gaming rarity review: "Rare (guaranteed 1 per pack), and Holo (approximately 1:3 packs)"; PokeBeach forum + PokeMaster base-set guide corroborate 1-in-3 holo for early sets base-rocket/gym). No per-set published study exists for Gym Challenge, so this maps the era-average WotC rate onto the set's buckets -> confidence LOW.

Slot mapping onto available buckets {rare-holo:20, rare:21, uncommon:42, common:49}:
- rare slot (1 card): holo 1/3 -> rare-holo 0.3333, otherwise non-holo rare 0.6667. Hit bucket = rare-holo.
- uncommon slot (3 cards): uncommon 1.0.
- common slot (7 cards): common 1.0. (WotC Gym boosters historically include energy in the card pool; this set's only matching bucket is common, so all 7 map to common.)
Each distribution sums to 1.0. All keys are a subset of the set's buckets.

Booster BOX = 36 packs (vintage collector item with large sealed premium; EV/booster is expected to sit well below box price — rates not inflated to compensate). NO ETB existed for this era -> products displayPacks/etbPacks = null.

Caveat: 0.3333+0.6667 rounds the 1/3 holo rate; effective rare-holo per pack ~0.333, i.e. ~1 holo every 3 packs as documented.
